disambiguation: This is not a benign autoscaler or CI burst because the principal (dev-user) has a high privilege level and the events are clustered closely together in time without proper tagging, indicating potential abuse rather than legitimate scaling activity.
key_evidence: 18 pod_create events detected from the same principal (dev-user) within a short time frame (less than 1 hour).
The principal (dev-user) has a high privilege level (2) and is part of the human_dev cohort.
The events occurred during regular business hours (not off-hours).
The tag completeness for the resources involved is 0.0, indicating a lack of proper ownership or tagging.
The risk score is high (0.850993329533245) and the risk band is classified as CRITICAL.
likely_intent: Malicious resource hijacking or abuse of privileges.
recommended_guardrails: Implement strict role-based access control (RBAC) to limit privileges for users in the dev cohort.
Enforce tagging policies for all resources to ensure proper ownership and accountability.
Set up alerts for unusual patterns of resource creation, especially from high-privilege accounts.
